behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the two required test functions (`force_relex_at_line_start_produces_thematic_break` and `setext_heading_in_blockquote`), the crates (`biome_markdown_parser`, `biome_parser`), and test suites (`--lib`, `--test spec_tests`).

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the bug (setext heading inside blockquote) without specifying the fix implementation; agent must discover the `force_relex_at_line_start` approach, though this name is leaked via the required test name.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The f2p test asserts existence of a test named `force_relex_at_line_start_produces_thematic_break`, which forces the agent to name an internal helper `force_relex_at_line_start` (gold-specific implementation detail); an alternative fix using a different helper name would fail this test.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute `cargo test`, `cargo clippy`, `cargo fmt`, and `cargo check` subprocesses rather than grepping files.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Clippy, fmt, check, and the two new tests are all covered by pytest cases matching the instruction.

informative_test_structure.reason: Each pytest function has a descriptive name and docstring explaining fail-to-pass vs pass-to-pass intent.

no_typos.reason: Paths, crate names, and command flags appear consistent between instruction and tests (`spec_tests` binary name matches).
